project_space.name: AI Explorer Framework
project_space.purpose: Working data space for evolving the AI Explorer Framework inside ChatGPT Projects before repo modularization
editing_rules: Prefer adding small structured blocks over rewriting large sections
Mark maturity clearly: raw, draft, tested, proven
Capture open questions instead of forcing premature clarity
Treat this as a living data space, not a polished publication

north_star.one_sentence: People adopt AI when discovery is delightful - make exploration irresistible and efficiency follows naturally

items.name: low_adoption_despite_training
items.statement: AI adoption stalls at 20-30% despite significant training investment

items.name: wrong_change_paradigm
items.statement: Organizations treat AI like enterprise software instead of like an exploratory medium

items.name: blank_chat_anxiety
items.statement: People do not know how to begin and freeze in front of open-ended AI interfaces

items.name: training_retention_failure
items.statement: Long trainings produce weak comprehension and weak transfer into practice

items.name: compliance_theater
items.statement: People perform adoption symbolically without building real confidence or practice

items.name: personal_fears_block_work_adoption
items.statement: Privacy fears, scam fears, and job fears spill into workplace adoption

items.name: expedition_style_discovery
items.statement: AI adoption works better when people are guided through safe, curiosity-driven exploration

items.name: knowledge_trails
items.statement: Discoveries become organizational capability when they are captured as reusable trails

items.name: community_practice
items.statement: Community rituals and peer sharing accelerate confidence and spread adoption patterns organically

items.name: delayed_productivity_metrics
items.statement: Productivity metrics should appear only after fascination and practice have taken root

items.name: fascination_before_efficiency
items.statement: Discovery creates intrinsic motivation; efficiency emerges after confidence

items.name: expedition_over_execution
items.statement: Safe wandering builds confidence; forced marches create resistance

items.name: harvest_curiosity_dont_manufacture_it
items.statement: People already want to explore AI; the task is to channel this energy productively

items.name: work_personal_integration
items.statement: Safe personal exploration supports transferable work confidence

items.name: trails_as_knowledge_artifacts
items.statement: Exploration becomes capability when it is documented and shared

items.name: creativity_unlocks_capability
items.statement: AI becomes more useful when people learn to vary perspective, constraints, and approaches
items.note: candidate new principle to develop further

phases.name: expedition
phases.purpose: Create conditions for fascination and capture discoveries

phases.name: harvest
phases.purpose: Sustain discovery as an ongoing practice and innovation system

stage_blocks.name: scale
stage_blocks.purpose: Transition proven patterns to enterprise adoption

items.name: champion_recruitment
items.purpose: Train guides who facilitate instead of teach

items.name: guided_expeditions
items.purpose: Generate structured exploration and new trails

items.name: discovery_showcases
items.purpose: Celebrate discoveries and attract more explorers

items.name: trail_capture_sprint
items.purpose: Convert discoveries into reusable knowledge artifacts

items.name: creative_exploration
items.why_now: Creativity is implicit in the framework but not yet explicit as a teachable capability

items.name: trail_feeds
items.purpose: Show what others discovered in related exploration paths

items.name: constraint_playgrounds
items.purpose: Use creative limitation to guide exploration

items.name: breadcrumb_trails
items.purpose: Visualize a user journey across explorations

items.name: discovery_wall
items.purpose: Create visible social proof through moments of surprise

trail_system.definition: A saved exploration path with context, discoveries, learnings, and next steps

work_personal_integration.core_claim: Personal AI fears block work adoption; safe personal exploration builds transferable confidence

items.name: learning_effectiveness
items.statement: 87% comprehension in 10-minute exploration vs 40% in 2-hour training

items.name: sustained_adoption
items.statement: 68% active usage at Month 12 vs 15% with traditional training

items.name: community_formation
items.statement: 40+ member self-organizing community

items.name: trail_library_growth
items.statement: 127 trails created, 30% reused 3+ times

items.name: pizza_agent_case
items.statement: Playful experiment became genuine workflow improvement and later training standard

priority_questions.question: How should creativity be integrated as a formal module in the framework?
Which creativity methods best fit AI adoption: design thinking, double diamond, lateral thinking, or lightweight hybrids?
How much structure supports creativity before it becomes restrictive?
What interface patterns create productive serendipity without overload?
What predicts explorer vs structure-seeker behavior in adoption journeys?
